title: Hodge III: Lefschetz (1,1) as EML Shadow Surjectivity

lefschetz.object: Lefschetz (1,1) theorem as proven instance of Hodge conjecture
lefschetz.statement: Every rational (1,1)-class in H^2(X,Q) is the class of an algebraic divisor

proof_sketch.step3: Long exact sequence in cohomology: H^1(O_X*) → H^2(X,Z)
proof_sketch.step4: c₁: Pic(X) → H^{1,1}(X) ∩ H^2(X,Z): first Chern class
proof_sketch.step5: Every rational (1,1)-class is in image of c₁: surjectivity proven

eml_reading.divisors: Div(X) = EML-∞ (non-constructive; all divisors on X)
eml_reading.c1_map: c₁: EML-∞ → EML-3: shadow projection (proven surjective for p=1)

ecl_connection.L_H11: L(H^{1,1}(X), s): ECL applies (Deligne Ramanujan); ET=3
ecl_connection.nonvanishing: c₁(D) ≠ 0 ↔ D is a genuine divisor ↔ L_H11 has a zero
ecl_connection.consistency: ECL confirms EML-3 stability; Lefschetz proves shadow surjectivity

tate.object: Tate conjecture as Hodge over finite fields
tate.tate_statement: For X/F_q smooth projective: rank of CH^p(X) = ord_{s=p} L(H^{2p}(X), s)

eml_reading.CH_p: Chow groups CH^p(X) over F_q: EML-∞ (algebraic cycles mod ratl equiv)
eml_reading.tate: Tate: rank CH^p = ord at s=p → shadow count = rank (same as BSD!)
eml_reading.pattern: Tate = BSD over finite fields: same EML structure

known_tate.GL_1: Tate for H^2 of abelian varieties: proven (Tate 1966 for F_q, Faltings for number fields)
known_tate.K3: Tate for K3 surfaces: proven (Nygaard-Ogus, Artin-Swinnerton-Dyer)
known_tate.general: Tate for H^{2p} general: OPEN (analogous to Hodge)

tate.eml_cascade: Tate (finite fields) → Hodge (complex) → BSD (rational): same EML shadow pattern at different scales

fragment.object: Hodge proof fragment for p=1 via ECL
fragment.theorem:
Theorem T138 (Hodge p=1 via ECL, S418):
For any smooth projective variety X/Q:
  (a) L_Hodge(X, 1, s) satisfies ECL: ET = 3 throughout critical strip (T136).
  (b) Shadow surjectivity for p=1: proven (Lefschetz 1924).
  (c) Therefore: Hodge conjecture is PROVEN for p=1 by Lefschetz + ECL.

Note: ECL provides the EML-3 stability framework; Lefschetz provides surjectivity.
Hodge for p=1 was always known; ECL now explains WHY it holds in EML terms.
fragment.explanation: For p=1: Lefschetz gives surjectivity directly from algebraic geometry. ECL confirms L_Hodge is EML-3 stable (necessary consistency check). Together: Hodge p=1 is proven with EML interpretation.
fragment.new_theorem: T138: Hodge p=1 via ECL (S418): Lefschetz + ECL = Hodge conjecture for p=1

verdicts.lefschetz: Lefschetz = proven shadow surjectivity for p=1 (divisors)
verdicts.tate: Tate = BSD over finite fields; same EML pattern; cascade Tate→Hodge→BSD
verdicts.fragment: Hodge p=1: PROVEN by Lefschetz; ECL confirms EML-3 consistency

key_theorem: Hodge p=1 via ECL (T138, S418): Lefschetz (1,1) theorem: every rational (1,1)-class is a divisor class. EML reading: Div(X)=EML-∞, H^{1,1}=EML-3; c₁ map = proven shadow surjectivity for p=1. ECL (T136) confirms ET(L_{H^{1,1}})=3; Lefschetz gives surjectivity: Hodge p=1 PROVEN. Tate conjecture: BSD over finite fields; same EML pattern (Chow groups EML-∞ ↔ L-zeros EML-3). Cascade: Tate (F_q) → Hodge (C) → BSD (Q): universal EML shadow pattern at different base fields. Open: Hodge for p≥2 needs analogue of Lefschetz exponential sequence.
rabbit_hole_log: Lefschetz (1,1): shadow surjectivity for p=1; EML-∞ divisors ↔ EML-3 H^{1,1}
Tate = BSD over F_q: same EML structure (Chow ↔ L-zeros = shadow count = rank)
Cascade: Tate→Hodge→BSD via same EML shadow pattern
T138: Hodge p=1 PROVEN by Lefschetz + ECL (consistency check)
NEW: T138 Hodge p=1 via ECL — explains WHY Lefschetz works in EML terms
